metadata.description: Persistent record of semantic validation rounds for MAgPIE-agent documentation quality tracking

schema_version_history.change: added top-level regression_questions array for stability tracking across rounds; rubric hoisted to feedback/flywheel_rubric.md

metadata.instructions: Append new rounds to the 'rounds' array. Each round must include all fields. Bug sources: 'doc_error' = bug in the AI documentation, 'answerer_confabulation' = agent invented wrong info despite docs being correct, 'ambiguous' = unclear which caused the error. Each new round MUST include at least 1 regression question from the top-level 'regression_questions' array (added 2026-05-23); score against expected_answer_summary; if regression, set drift_observed=true and update used_in_rounds. Severity tiers + audit format: feedback/flywheel_rubric.md.

regression_questions.question: What is the default realization of module 14 (yields)? List the equations defined in its equations.gms.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: Default realization: managementcalib_aug19 (verify via 'grep cfg$gms$yields ../config/default.cfg'). Equations defined in equations.gms: exactly 2 — q14_yield_crop (crop yields) and q14_yield_past (pasture yields). Verify exact line ranges against modules/14_yields/managementcalib_aug19/equations.gms. (Note: 'q14_yieldcalib' was historically listed in this anchor but does NOT exist as a defined equation — corrected 2026-05-24 R22 after audit.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests whether the agent (a) consults default.cfg before describing the realization, (b) reads the actual equations file rather than reconstructing from memory, (c) reports the equation count correctly (Bug_Taxonomy.md Pattern 6 -- Hardcoded counts drift)

regression_questions.question: Walk through how vm_carbon_stock is computed in Module 52 and where it enters the GHG-policy cost in Module 56. Cite the relevant equations and file:line locations.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: vm_carbon_stock is DECLARED in Module 56 (price_aug22/declarations.gms:34) — NOT in Module 52. Land modules (29 cropland, 31 pasture, 32 forestry, 34 urban, 35 natveg) and Module 59 (SOM, soilc pool) POPULATE the variable. Module 52 only READS vm_carbon_stock and pcm_carbon_stock to compute CO2 emissions via q52_emis_co2_actual (normal_dec17/equations.gms:16-19). Module 56's chain: q56_emis_pricing_co2 (equations.gms:19-22) reads (pcm - vm)/timestep_length → v56_emis_pricing → q56_emission_cost_oneoff (equations.gms:45-52) → v56_emission_cost → q56_emission_costs (equations.gms:56-58) → vm_emission_costs(i). The %c56_carbon_stock_pricing% switch default is 'actualNoAcEst'. Citations must use full relative paths per MANDATE 16. (Corrected 2026-05-24 R22 — earlier summary incorrectly attributed declaration to M52.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) cross-module dependency tracing, (b) precise file:line citations, (c) variable-name fidelity (no confabulation of related-sounding names like vm_carbon_stocks), (d) gate-tabulation behavior when the chain has multiple branches, (e) producer-vs-consumer distinction (which module DECLARES vs which only READS the variable)

root_causes: Interesting Over Default (~30%): agent describes non-default realizations as if active
Plausible Confabulation (~25%): agent invents formulas/mechanisms that sound right
Documentation Drift (~20%): docs have stale info from older MAgPIE versions
Drive-by module characterizations: wrong descriptions of tangentially-mentioned modules
Residual documentation drift in less-visited modules
safeguards_added: Anti-confabulation rule 6: never characterize a module you haven't just looked up

root_causes: Massive answerer confabulation (~85%): agent invents plausible variable names (vm_water_available, vm_water_demand, pcm_AEI) and defaults despite docs being correct
Bimodal pattern: previously-validated modules (Q3,Q4,Q5) scored 7.5 avg; never-validated (Q1,Q2) scored 3.25
Variable name invention: agent constructs 'vm_water_*' names following MAgPIE naming patterns but none exist
Default guessing: agent guesses realization names using naming patterns (e.g. fbask_aug21) instead of looking up docs
safeguards_added: Anti-confabulation rule 7: never construct variable names from patterns

bug_details.desc: Used shorthand q16_supply instead of actual q16_supply_crops/q16_supply_livestock etc
Conflated s32_max_aff_area=Inf with derived p32_max_aff_area_glo which is 0 in historic periods
Implies residues flow through trade but kres are k_notrade commodities
Equation q21_excess_prod does not exist - actual name is q21_excess_supply
i21_exp_shr dimension t should be t_all
wat_src set member order wrong (ren_ground and technical swapped)
Groundwater safety valve description imprecise - compares against all wat_src not just surface
v35_hvarea does not exist - actual is v35_hvarea_primforest(j), v35_hvarea_secdforest(j,ac), v35_hvarea_other(j,othertype35,ac)
nothing→primforest=0 is ambiguous natural language in context of specific set element references
vm_land.lo primforest described as max() but code uses conditional $ replacement

root_causes: Anti-confabulation rules dramatically effective: 82% reduction in confabulation bugs (44→8)
Remaining confabulations are qualitatively different: shortcuts/truncations rather than fabrications
Zero Critical bugs for the first time across 4 rounds
Module 21 (trade) most error-prone: 4 of 11 bugs, including the only Major confabulation (q21_excess_prod→q21_excess_supply)
safeguards_added: Anti-confabulation rules 7-9 injected into answerer prompts
GAMS reference docs promoted to auto-load triggers in AGENT.md

critical_bugs: Answerer omitted t=1 vm_land.fx logic (docs already correct)
questions.root_cause: Answerer failed to relay documented presolve conditional logic
Answerer missed 3/6 M71 equations and got R script algorithm inventory wrong despite docs having correct info
Structural comparison oversimplified; dimension shifts and semantic parameter changes not fully explained. Doc gap: lacked explicit comparison table (now fixed).
GWP AR4 vs AR5 conflation (docs already note both); enteric formula oversimplified (docs have full detail)
Perfect score — prior validation rounds fixed all N-cycle doc issues

summary.key_insight: Paradigm shift: 0 doc errors found. All 16 bugs are answerer errors (75% incomplete, 25% confabulation). Docs are now accurate — the bottleneck has moved from doc quality to answerer reading comprehension. Only M21 needed a structural comparison table addition.

bugs.description: Equation count: claimed 22 in m15_food_demand model block, actual is 17
Fabricated quote from realization.gms — paraphrased content presented as direct citation
States sugar is only freely-traded secondary product; molasses also not in k_hardtrade21
Called cost multiplier an 'annuity factor' — actual formula is capital recovery factor (pm_interest+depreciation)/(1+pm_interest)
Omitted that water demand LHS is vm_watdem*v42_irrig_eff; livestock term uses vm_prod not vm_area with asymmetric efficiency application
q44_cost LHS is sum(cell(i2,j2), vm_cost_bv_loss(j2)), not bare vm_cost_bv_loss — spatial summation omitted
Unit for s44_cost_bii_missing stated as USD17MER but full unit is USD17MER per BII unit

summary.key_insight: Best round ever (8.7 mean). Zero doc errors for second consecutive round. All 7 bugs are answerer reading comprehension issues: equation simplification (3), numerical miscounts (1), fabricated quotes (1), terminology imprecision (2). Documentation is now highly accurate across all tested modules.

systemic_patterns: Wrong line references for dependent modules
Fabricated code examples (7 in one doc)
Wrong diet mode interaction (c15_EAT_scen ignored in diet=3)
Wrong file extensions/paths for input data

rounds.scope: GAMS language docs (vs official GAMS docs)

systemic_patterns: Wrong inline comment syntax (* vs !! throughout)

rounds.notes: Re-tested previously-weak modules post-fix. M14/M29 still weakest (realization confusion, fabricated counts). M52/53/56/58 near-perfect. Plus meta-check found missing M45 in DOT graph and 5 stale counts.

questions.topic: Labor supply → employment → factor costs → objective function
questions.key_finding: Module 38 described as sticky_labor but default is sticky_feb18; Module 37 described as exo but default is off. Entire CES causal chain doesn't exist in default config.
Perfect score — module 54 stub correctly documented, P infrastructure in M18/M55 accurately described.

questions.topic: Age classes, timber harvesting, forestry lifecycle
questions.key_finding: Core mechanics correct. Line number drift (off by 6 lines in M32), GDP vs GDP per capita conflation, missing wood_fuel elasticity exception.

questions.topic: Urban land expansion defaults and conservation interactions
questions.key_finding: Core urban mechanics well-documented. Conservation type oversimplification, static realization description imprecise, BII aggregation context misleading.
s62_include_bioplastic defaults to 1 (ON), not 0 (OFF). s62_historical file reference wrong.

root_causes: Interesting Over Default (M37 off→exo, M38 sticky_feb18→sticky_labor): 2 bugs, Critical+Major
Wrong default value (M62 bioplastic ON not OFF): 1 Major bug
Line number drift (M32 presolve.gms shifted ~6 lines): 3 Minor bugs
Oversimplification of complex mechanisms: 2 Minor bugs
Plausible confabulation (GDP vs GDP/cap, conservation types): remaining
summary.validator_status: 32/32 checks pass after fixes (778 vars, 269 eqs, 68 realizations, 2233 citations)

questions.question: What is the default interest rate in MAgPIE and how is it calculated? Trace exactly how pm_interest enters the factor cost equations (module 38) and land conversion cost equations (module 39).
How does climate change data flow into MAgPIE? Trace the path from module 45_climate through yield adjustments (module 14) to cropland allocation (modules 29, 30).
Explain how MAgPIE disaggregates livestock production in module 71. How does feed demand from module 70 connect to the demand module (15/16)?
How do MACCs work in MAgPIE? What is the relationship between module 57, module 53, and module 56? How does a carbon price translate into emission reductions?
What determines the cost of converting land between types? How does module 39 calculate costs? How does this interact with the land balance (module 10) and what causes infeasibility?

bugs.description: Wrong line numbers for M38 presolve.gms capital need computation (21-22 vs 25-26)
Fabricated line numbers for q38_cost_prod_capital (46-49 vs 21-24; file only 45 lines)
Claims 13 use sites across 7 modules but lists 9 modules — arithmetic error
M70 default fbask_jan16 does NOT use pm_interest; cited equations only in non-default fbask_jan16_sticky
q56_reward_cdr_aff formula described with + but actual is multiplication x annuity
f14_yields line number wrong (input.gms:44 vs 35-37)
Docs claim all 4 consumers use weighted-average pattern; M58/M59 use mapping/aggregation instead
sm_fix_cc controls 7 modules not 6 — missing M35 (natveg/pot_forest_may24)
Unverifiable empirical claim about yield change magnitudes
kli_rum/kli_rd presented as synonyms but have different parent sets
Demand chain arrow direction misleading (M70 feeds INTO M16)
Cost integral described as trapezoid sum but is actually rectangular/Riemann sum
Claims 13 slack variables — number fabricated
200 cells stated as fixed architectural constant; actually depends on spatial resolution
Docs said NET transitions but matrix captures GROSS between-type transitions
s31_fac_req_past described as calibration vs standard runs; actually first timestep vs subsequent
Default realization is nlp_apr17, NOT lp_nlp_apr17 (Interesting Over Default antipattern)
Reward condition described as runtime GAMS check but is offline-derived calibration data
Incomplete: calibration applies to both cost AND reward dimensions
Infeasibility risk ranking unverifiable from GAMS code

doc_fixes_applied: module_80.md: default realization nlp_apr17 not lp_nlp_apr17 (Interesting Over Default #3)
module_10.md: net transitions → gross/between-type transitions (8 locations)
module_10.md: 200 cells → configurable spatial resolution (2 locations)
module_45.md: consumer pattern differentiated (weighted avg vs mapping)
key_patterns: Interesting Over Default antipattern strikes again (M80: lp_nlp_apr17 labeled default, actual is nlp_apr17)
Fabricated line numbers remain the #1 answerer confabulation pattern
Module 10 had semantic imprecision (net vs gross) propagated across 8 doc locations

rounds.note: First round using Opus as answerer model (R1-R15 used Sonnet 4.6). Scores not directly comparable to prior rounds.

questions.question_summary: Urban land expansion: default realization, equations, deviation cost, LUH3 data, land balance and biodiversity interactions

bugs.description: Fabricated expanded q10_land_area equation — actual code uses set-based sum
Wrote 't = 1' instead of GAMS ordinal 'ord(t) = 1'
Static realization: realization.gms says LUH2 but default.cfg says LUH3

questions.question_summary: Phosphorus module disabled: default realization, vm_p_fert_costs=0, zero equations, M50 nr-only tracking, M55 P recycling at 100%

bugs.description: Added 'phosphorus' to vm_p_fert_costs description — code just says 'mineral fertilizers'

questions.question_summary: Age class lifecycle: set definition, GFAD remapping, shift logic, rotation constraints, timber demand, Chapman-Richards growth

bugs.description: Claimed age classes go to ac140,acx — actual set extends to ac300 (62 elements). Docs are correct at line 443.
class15 described as '140+ years' in 6 locations — code comment says '150 years or older'. FIXED.
Omitted wood_fuel exception to income elasticity zeroing — docs cover it at line 148
Showed only endogenous harvest mode — docs cover all 3 modes (s32_hvarea=0,1,2) at lines 793-810
GFAD mapping only populates ac5-ac140 and acx; ac0 and ac145-ac300 stay at 0

questions.question_summary: Employment chain: GDP-wage regression, labor productivity, crop/livestock factor costs, employment back-calculation

bugs.description: Showed i38_fac_req and pm_factor_cost_shares as separate factors — code pre-bakes them into p38_labor_need
Wage/productivity terms shown outside sum(kcr) — in code they're inside nested sums

questions.question_summary: Manure N flow: AWMS balance, 4 categories, confinement→N₂O/CH₄ emissions, pasture emissions, manure recycling to cropland N budget

bugs.description: Said Ym=0.03 for 'beef concentrates' — applies to all non-dairy ruminants (livst_rum)
Grouped Ym=0.065 as 'dairy/non-conc.' — actually 3 distinct terms

rounds.note: First round after Check 18 (default realization validator) and anti-confabulation rules 10-12. Confab rate dropped to 22%. Found additional tropical rewetted EF errors beyond audit scope.

questions.question: Trace the SSP driver chain: What default SSP scenario does MAgPIE use? How do module 09 population/GDP projections flow into module 12 interest rate calculation? What is the default realization of module 80 and how does the optimization type affect solver behavior?

Quantify the GHG impact of peatland rewetting: What are the exact emission factor values for boreal rewetted peatlands (CO2, DOC, CH4, N2O) in module 58? How does module 45 climate data feed into module 52 carbon stock calculations? What is the phosphorus budget approach in module 54?

Trace the material demand chain: How does module 62 calculate bioplastic demand? What happens when s62_include_bioplastic is switched on/off? How does material demand flow through module 20 processing to module 17 production costs and module 60 bioenergy?

Edge case: What does s71_lp_fix do in module 71 livestock disaggregation? How does module 70 handle feed demand when livestock mix changes? What are the transport cost equations in module 40? How does module 16 handle demand elasticities at extreme price changes?

Conservation law: How does module 34 urban land expansion interact with module 10 land balance? What land types are in the land_consv set in module 22? How does module 39 landconversion calculate costs and what are the key cost parameters?

rounds.retest_bias_note: RE-TEST ROUND: Questions reused from R15-R16. Docs may have been specifically fixed for issues these questions originally exposed. Scores are biased upward for doc-related improvements but unbiased for confabulation-related changes.

questions.question: What is the default interest rate in MAgPIE and how is it calculated? Trace exactly how pm_interest enters the factor cost equations (module 38) and land conversion cost equations (module 39).
What determines the cost of converting land between types? How does module 39 calculate costs? How does this interact with the land balance (module 10) and what causes infeasibility?

Trace the complete age-class lifecycle in MAgPIE: How is the ac set defined? How does GFAD remapping work? Explain the shift logic, rotation constraints, timber demand interaction (module 73), and Chapman-Richards growth parameters (module 32, module 52).
How does climate change data flow into MAgPIE? Trace the path from module 45_climate through yield adjustments (module 14) to cropland allocation (modules 29, 30).

Trace the employment chain in MAgPIE: How does the GDP-wage regression work (module 36)? How is labor productivity calculated (module 37)? How do crop and livestock factor costs incorporate labor (module 38, module 70)? How is employment back-calculated?

summary.key_finding: Documentation is essentially error-free for re-tested questions (0 doc_errors). 95% of bugs are answerer confabulation. Score improvement (+0.9) driven by Q2 (+2.0) and Q3 (+2.5) where doc fixes directly addressed original failures. Q1 regressed (-0.5) due to new confabulations (fabricated realization name, wrong realization code).

questions.question: What equations does the default realization of Module 38 (factor_costs) use? Show the complete equation set with their GAMS names, mathematical formulations, and explain the difference between the default and the sticky_labor alternative. Be specific about which equations exist in one realization but not the other.

In Module 71 (disagg_lvst), what is the default realization and how does its forage-based livestock disaggregation work? What are the key equations? How does the foragebased_aug18 alternative differ from the default foragebased_jul23? What happens when the off realization is selected?
questions.note: Auditor incorrectly flagged default as off (auditor error — misread realization description comments). 2 doc fixes: M71 aug18 differences corrected (had division-by-zero protection, different equation structure).

questions.question: Explain how Module 80 (optimization) works. What is the default realization and what solver does it use? How does the lp_nlp_apr17 alternative differ — what does it do in the LP presolve phase vs the NLP phase? What are the key switches and parameters that control optimization behavior?

How does Module 41 (area_equipped_for_irrigation) determine how much irrigated area is available? What is the default realization's approach? How does the endogenous investment equation work — what are the costs and constraints? How does the static alternative differ? Trace how vm_area_equipped_for_irrigation connects to Module 42 (water_demand) and Module 30 (croparea).

What is the default realization of Module 59 (som — soil organic matter) and how does it model SOC dynamics? Show the key equations. How do the cellpool_aug16 and static_jan19 alternatives differ? What input data drives SOC calculations and how do land-use transitions affect soil carbon? How does Module 59 connect to the carbon balance through Module 56?
questions.note: cellpool_aug16 removed from model (no code files, only input/ dir remains). Doc updated to reflect this.

key_findings: All 5 questions were default_switch archetype targeting our new callout blocks
0 wrong-realization confabulations (Tier 1 target) — callout blocks appear effective
Highest mean score in any round (8.4/10)
1 auditor error: misread realization comments as default setting for M71
Found cellpool_aug16 is deprecated (removed from module.gms) — doc updated
Found M71 aug18 section incorrectly described differences — doc corrected

rounds.trigger: post-sync targeted validation: 2026-04-20 sync of PR #869 forestry/timber/carbon overhaul updated 6 module docs (14,29,32,35,52,73), which exceeds the 3-module threshold

questions.question: Describe end-to-end what happens when s52_growingstock_calib = 1 (the default as of 2026-04-20). From loading FRA 2025 targets, through bisection in M52 preloop, to how calibrated Chapman-Richards k flows into M14's im_growing_stock and ultimately M32/M35 production equations. Covers: input files, interface params, bisection mechanics (bounds, iterations), what's overwritten vs preserved, M14 presolve formula, M32/M35 equation usage, switch-off behavior, uncalib variant consumers.
questions.notes: Major bug: Q1-B1 — listed only M29 as _uncalib consumer; missed M32 afforestation/NDC (which is explicitly documented in M52 realization.gms). 5 line-number drifts in preloop.gms/start.gms citations (pre-sync docs were generated from a slightly different snapshot). 1 wrong date (pm_land_plantation 'new 2026-04-20' but PR #869 merged 2026-03-16). 1 terminology issue ('superset' for sm_carbon_fraction). 2 oversimplifications (shared C_max for plantation bisection; 'fixed LPJmL' for primforest under cc scenario).

questions.question: Walk through how per-m3 roundwood price (s73_timber_prod_cost_wood = 89 USD17MER/m3) becomes regional per-tDM costs entering the objective function. Covers: default values and unit change; preloop conversion via im_vol_conv; FAO stacking correction; 4-term cost equation structure including natveg premium as differential; q32_cost_establishment flow (PV of future harvest); residue equation before/after rewrite.
questions.notes: 5 line-number drifts (preloop.gms:41/42, :89-90/:90-91, equations.gms:16-27/:23-31, residue :72-79/:75-81, M32 :157-172/:166-173). Wrong date (Q2-B6, 'dimensionality change 2026-04-20' but actually 2026-03-21 in commit 896a9b728). Oversimplification of pre-update residue formula (Q2-B7). Stacking overstatement framing ~54% vs doc's 35% (Q2-B8 nitpick). Mechanistic content all correct — no fabricated variables or equations.

questions.question: On cropland tree cover (Module 29), how does the model decide which carbon density growth curve to use, and why? Covers: default realization + s29_treecover_plantation; origin and phase of _uncalib variants; what's overwritten vs preserved under calibration; rationale for uncalib in new establishment; M29-M52 preloop sequencing; M28's role.
questions.notes: Line-number drifts: start.gms:33-34 should be 43-44, preloop.gms:66-69 should be 71-73, start.gms:17-38 is imprecise range. 'af_ndc realization' is mischaracterization (realization is dynamic_may24; aff/ndc are sub-types). 'No FRA target for other' inferred rationale stated as fact (Q3-B5 nitpick). All conceptual claims correct: default realization, switch behavior, _uncalib origin/phase/rationale, sequencing, M28 role.

bugs_fixed_this_session: Q1-B1 (Major, doc_error): Added M32 afforestation/NDC to _uncalib consumer list in module_52.md Section 2.C and parameter entries 1b/3b
Q1-B2/B3/B4/B5/B6 (Minor, drift): Corrected line citations in module_52.md Section 2.C (preloop.gms:21, 28-33, 45-73, 71-73, 84-116, 108-113, 114-116; start.gms:40, 43-44)
Q1-B8 (Minor, confabulation): Replaced 'superset scalar' with 'shared/interface scalar' in module_14.md and module_52.md
Q3-B2/B3 (Minor, drift): Corrected start.gms and preloop.gms line numbers in module_52.md parameter descriptions
bugs_deferred: Q1-B7, Q2-B6 (Minor, date): 'new 2026-04-20' wording where commit was actually 2026-03-16 — the 2026-04-20 is the SYNC date, not the code-change date. Low impact; context is clear.

Q1-B9, Q1-B10, Q2-B7, Q2-B8, Q3-B4, Q3-B5 (Minor/Nitpick, oversimplifications): Shared C_max for plantation bisection; 'fixed LPJmL' for primforest; pre-update residue formula simplification; stacking framing; start.gms:17-38 imprecise range; inferred 'no FRA target' rationale. All defensible; worth documenting in a follow-up pass if user wants.
key_findings: Mean score 8.17/10 — within 'Mostly Accurate' band. All three questions scored 7.5-8.5, indicating consistent quality after the 2026-04-20 sync.
Zero Critical or Fundamentally Flawed answers. No fabricated variables, no wrong equation names, no wrong realizations.

Dominant bug class: file:line citation drift (13/23 = 57%). This matches the historic pattern (71% in Bug_Taxonomy). Likely caused by drafting docs against one snapshot then not re-verifying citations after minor commits restructured the code. Mitigation: for citations in post-sync doc updates, derive lines from the final merged origin/develop, not from an intermediate commit.

One Major doc_error: M52 uncalib consumer list missed M32 afforestation/NDC. Rationale: my sync focused on the M29 tree-cover addition and didn't audit which OTHER modules had always consumed uncalib variants. Mitigation: when adding '_uncalib' parameters, grep the whole codebase for all consumers (grep `-uncalib` across modules/**/*.gms).
Answerer confabulation rate: 30% of bugs (7/23) — well within the 22-33% range of recent targeted rounds. The answerer reliably drew from the docs but introduced minor imprecisions.

Validation pipeline improvement: the first round of the syntactic validator post-sync flagged 27 GAMS variable mismatches, all expected (new variables on develop not yet in local tree). After git pull origin develop, 10 remained — all historical references in 'formerly X' context, which the checker couldn't distinguish. Resolved by switching backtick wrappers to italics for deprecated names. This is a reusable pattern for future sync cycles.

rounds.scope: Post-sync targeted validation of the 6 modules updated in the 2026-05-16 develop sync (PR #866 trade, #871 nlp_ipopt, #876 secdforest carbon)

questions.question: After PR #866's bilateral-trade overhaul, name the three trade-cost interface variables and their defining Module 21 equations, explain what each represents, explain how Module 11 aggregates them, and name the former single variable and the default trade realization.
questions.notes: All substantive claims correct: 3 variables (vm_cost_trade_tariff/margin/feasibility), 3 equations, formulas, M11 aggregation, former vm_cost_trade, selfsuff_reduced default, defaults s21_cost_import=1500 and s21_min_trade_margin_forestry=62. Q1-B1 (Major, answerer_confabulation): answer framed PR #866 as a clean rename of an equation pair, understating that one combined per-commodity cost stream was split into three and the objective-facing constraint changed from =e= to =g=. Q1-B2 (Minor): v11_cost_reg/vm_cost_glo objective path not spelled out. Q1-B3 (Nitpick): 'sums 32 cost terms' loose since vm_reward_cdr_aff is subtracted.

questions.question: Is nlp_ipopt the default optimization realization? What solver does it use vs the default, how does it handle option files, and what is its retry/re-solve behavior?
questions.notes: Correct: nlp_ipopt non-default (default nlp_apr17), Ipopt vs CONOPT4, dynamic two-file put/putclose, ipopt.op2 written-but-never-activated, no s80_resolve_option cycling, scalar inventory. Q2-B1 (Major, doc_clarity): answer claimed nlp_apr17 does NOT write option files dynamically and uses a static conopt4.opt. False: nlp_apr17 also writes conopt4.opt via put/putclose; the real difference is conopt4.op2 via compile-time $onecho. module_80.md's 'Unlike the CONOPT-based realizations...' framing invited the misread; fixed this session. Q2-B2/B3 (Minor): retry-loop and default.cfg comment-block line ranges slightly off.

questions.question: How does Module 35 track natural-origin secondary forest for carbon density (parameters, blend formula, the equation and its density argument)? In Module 52, how does the growing-stock bisection determine Chapman-Richards k and what bounds it?
questions.notes: All load-bearing claims correct: p35_secdforest_natural / pc35_secdforest_natural / p35_carbon_density_secdforest, the blend formula, q35_carbon_secdforest density argument, the 25-iteration bisection, s52_k_high_secdf=0.1 and s52_k_high_plant=0.15, hardcoded 0.001 lower bound, replaced-0.3 history. Q3-B2 (Minor)/Q3-B3 (Nitpick): two citation ranges start on comment lines. Q3-B4 (Nitpick): 'FRA 2025 target' over-specifies the parameter name. Key missing nuance (added to module_52.md): the bisection has no convergence test, so k clamps to a bound if the FRA target is unreachable.

bugs_fixed_this_session: Q2-B1 (Major, doc_clarity): rewrote module_80.md's CONOPT-vs-Ipopt option-file contrast and the realization-comparison table row. CONOPT realizations also write conopt4.opt via put/putclose; the real difference is conopt4.op2 ($onecho) vs ipopt.op2 (put).
Q1-B1 (Major): reworded module_21.md's equation note to say PR #866 SPLIT one combined trade-cost stream into three, not a clean rename of an equation pair.
module_52.md: removed a confusing 'lower bound s52_k_high_secdf' phrase (it is an UPPER bound) and added a 'no convergence test' caveat to the bisection section (the Q3 missing nuance).
bugs_deferred: Q1-B2/B3, Q2-B2/B3, Q3-B2/B3/B4 (Minor/Nitpick): objective-path incompleteness, citation ranges that begin on comment lines, and 'FRA 2025' naming. Low impact, mostly answerer-side imprecision. Recorded, not separately fixed.
key_findings: Mean 8.0/10, 'Mostly Accurate' band, consistent with the post-sync R20 result (8.17). Zero Critical bugs; no fabricated variables, equations, or realizations in any of the three answers.

Both Major bugs were contrast/causality framing errors, not factual fabrication: the answerer compressed a 'split into three' into a 'rename', and over-generalized a doc's 'static $onecho for .op2' into 'static option files'. The two doc passages that invited those misreads were tightened.
First round whose docs validate 33/33 with the hardened tooling (the validator set -e crash and the equation-checker italics-blindness were both found and fixed during the preceding sync).

Q3 surfaced a genuine model behavior worth documenting: Module 52's growing-stock bisection is a fixed 25-iteration loop with no convergence test, so k clamps to a bound when the FRA target is unreachable. Caveat added to module_52.md.

rounds.commit_after: pending (R22 fixes recorded together with this round entry)
rounds.scope: First round under schema v1.1 — includes G1 and G2 regression questions plus 4 new probes designed to test R3 + R4 fixes (M11 §17.2 + §3, M70 vm_costs_additional_mon attribution, land conservation, AEI default).

questions.question: Trace how a unit of phosphorus-fertilizer cost enters MAgPIE's objective function. Name the variable; producing module; consuming module + equation; show form and file:line.
questions.notes: Sonnet correctly identified vm_p_fert_costs, M54 attribution (not M50/M51), the 'off' realization-only and .fx(i)=0 in preloop, the q11_cost_reg consumption, and the q11_cost_glo objective aggregation. Q1-B1 (Major, answerer_confabulation): Sonnet cited 'equations.gms:93' for vm_nr_inorg_fert_costs and 'equations.gms:94' for vm_p_fert_costs — actual lines are 24 and 25 (the equations.gms file is only 69 lines total). Sonnet appears to have confused the doc's module_11.md line numbers (the cost vars appear at L93/94 of the doc) with equations.gms line numbers. Internally inconsistent — same answer correctly cited the q11_cost_reg block as spanning equations.gms:15-47.

questions.question: Examine vm_costs_additional_mon: dimensions; producer; meaning of 'additional_mon'; entry into M11; M70 vs M71 attribution.
questions.notes: Sonnet correctly identified 1D dimensions, M71 attribution (not M70), 'additional monogastric' meaning (not monitoring), the s71_punish_additional_mon=15000 calibration, the q71_punishment_mon aggregation, M11 entry at equations.gms:40. Q2-B1 (Major, doc_error introduced in R3 commit ae3763f): Sonnet cited the variable's declaration at 'modules/71_disagg_lvst/foragebased_aug18/declarations.gms:11' — but the default realization is foragebased_jul23 (verified in config/default.cfg). The R3 fix in module_70.md and module_11.md §17.2 introduced foragebased_aug18 references; both fixed this session in R22 cleanup. Internally inconsistent — Sonnet correctly named foragebased_jul23 elsewhere in same answer.

questions.question: Land conservation: which equation; mathematical form; module; set; failure if bioenergy_plantation added; equality vs inequality.
questions.notes: Perfect answer. Sonnet correctly identified q10_land_area, exact file:line (modules/10_land/landmatrix_dec18/equations.gms:13-15), the strict-equality form with sum(land,...) preserved (NOT enumerated — explicitly avoided the R16 anchor bug), correct land-set membership (7 members) and definition location (core/sets.gms:250-251), and a technically sound failure-mechanism analysis for adding a new land type. Mechanical-check misses (M4 epistemic badges, M6 closing source statement) noted but not scored as bugs per rubric §2.

questions.question: Module 41 (AEI): realizations + default; endogenous vs exogenous by default; cfg switch + default value; consequence for expansion; downstream modules; how to switch.
questions.notes: Sonnet correctly identified 2 realizations (endo_apr13 default + static), the cfg switch + default value, the vm_AEI.lo (endo) vs vm_AEI.fx (static) distinction, q41_area_irrig coupling to M30 vm_area, q41_cost_AEI in endo_apr13 only, s41_AEI_depreciation=0 → irreversibility, switching procedure. Q4-B1 (Minor, framing): M42 attribution loose — M42 reads vm_area(j,kcr,'irrigated') directly, not vm_AEI. M41 affects M42 only indirectly via the q41_area_irrig cap on vm_area. Substantively correct framing; just imprecise about mechanism.

questions.question: What is the default realization of Module 14 (Yields)? List equations defined in equations.gms with names + descriptions.
questions.regression_flag: expected_answer_summary was WRONG (claimed 3 equations including q14_yieldcalib which does not exist); Sonnet correctly identified 2 equations (q14_yield_crop, q14_yield_past). Anchor corrected this session; drift_observed: false (Sonnet correct against ground truth).
questions.notes: Sonnet verified all claims: default managementcalib_aug19 (only realization), exactly 2 equations q14_yield_crop (eq.gms:14-16) and q14_yield_past (eq.gms:35-39), correct formulas, s14_yld_past_switch=0.25 default. Also caught an internal doc inconsistency (two cite ranges for q14_yield_past). G1 expected_answer_summary in validation_rounds.json corrected to remove the spurious q14_yieldcalib reference.

questions.question: Walk through how vm_carbon_stock is computed in M52 and where it enters the GHG-policy cost in M56. Cite equations and file:line.
questions.regression_flag: expected_answer_summary was WRONG (claimed M52 defines vm_carbon_stock; actually M56 declares it, M52 only reads). Sonnet had the producer/consumer distinction CORRECT — better than the anchor. Anchor rewritten this session; drift_observed: true (drift was in anchor, not Sonnet).
questions.notes: Sonnet correctly identified the producer/consumer distinction (M56 declares vm_carbon_stock, M52 only reads), the q52_emis_co2_actual chain, the M56 chain (q56_emis_pricing_co2 → v56_emis_pricing → q56_emission_cost_oneoff → v56_emission_cost → q56_emission_costs → vm_emission_costs), and the %c56_carbon_stock_pricing% default 'actualNoAcEst'. G2-B1/B2/B3 (Minor each, answerer_confabulation, auxiliary list errors): listed M30 as populator (actual: M29 — M30 declares vm_carbon_stock_croparea which is a separate variable M29 uses); listed M58 as populator (actual: M58 does NOT populate vm_carbon_stock; peatland flows through separate path); omitted M59 (SOM populates the soilc pool). The cited equation chain is correct — these errors are in the contributing-modules auxiliary list, not the high-stakes citation chain.

regression_anchors_corrected: G1 expected_answer_summary: removed spurious q14_yieldcalib reference (equation does not exist in any module 14 realization)
G2 expected_answer_summary: corrected M52-as-declarer to M56-as-declarer; added M59 (SOM) to populator list; removed M58; clarified that M52 only READS vm_carbon_stock
bugs_fixed_this_session: Q2-B1 (Major, doc_error): module_70.md L712 + module_11.md L496: changed 'modules/71_disagg_lvst/foragebased_aug18/declarations.gms:11' → 'modules/71_disagg_lvst/foragebased_jul23/declarations.gms:11' (the actual default realization). Bug was introduced in R3 commit ae3763f.
bugs_deferred: Q1-B1 (answerer_confabulation, lines 93/94 vs 24/25): not a doc bug. Sonnet confused doc line numbers with source line numbers. No doc fix needed.
G2-B1/B2/B3 (answerer auxiliary-list errors): not doc bugs — these are Sonnet attribution errors. No doc fix needed.
Q4-B1 (framing): could be addressed by tightening module_41.md's downstream description but not strictly wrong.
highlights: First round under schema v1.1 (regression_questions G1 + G2 included alongside 4 new probes).
Mean score 8.67/10 (Mostly Accurate band; comparable to R6's 8.7 high water mark).
Zero Critical bugs — first time both regression anchors AND new probes had no Criticals. R3+R3-followup+R4 mechanizations are holding.
Q3 (land conservation) was a perfect 10/10. Sonnet explicitly avoided the R16 anchor bug (preserved sum notation instead of enumerating set members).

Both regression anchors (G1, G2) had expected_answer_summary errors that Sonnet OUTPERFORMED. This is a strong signal — the anchors themselves needed correction; agent's actual capability exceeded the calibration baseline.
5/6 R3+R4-doc-fix-targeted probes scored ≥8 — confirms the prior session fixes hold and the docs are accurate enough for downstream answer quality.

rounds.commit_after: pending (R23 fixes recorded together with this round entry)
rounds.scope: Second round under schema v1.1 — designed to test migration-touched docs: cross_module/* (128 citation rewrites across 12 conservation/safety/circular-dependency docs), agent/helpers/* (5 helpers migrated, scenario_diet_change.md alone had 29 conversions), AGENT.md format-example updates, plus G1+G2 regression anchors. Probes intentionally exercise post-migration helpers (debugging_infeasibility, water_scarcity_scenarios, scenario_diet_change, interpreting_outputs) and the cross_module conservation docs.

questions.question: Trace s15_exo_diet=3 (recommended EAT-Lancet bounds mode) ruminant demand reduction through to soil N budget. (a) M15 mechanism; (b) M15→M16 interface variable + dimensions; (c) M16 aggregation equation for ruminant kap + file:line; (d) M17 regional-production variable + how produced; (e) q50_nr_bal_crp form + file:line; (f) one M51 downstream equation.
questions.notes: Q1-B1 (Major, doc_error propagation): Sonnet faithfully reproduced module_17.md's stale claim that 'vm_prod(j,k) for livestock remains zero' / 'vm_prod_reg(i, livst_rum) sums to zero'. This was true pre-M71 foragebased_jul23 (default since 2023) but is now wrong — M71's q71_feed_forage and q71_prod_mon_liv constrain vm_prod(j, kli_rum) and vm_prod(j, kli_mon), so vm_prod_reg(i, kli) is non-zero in default config. The source comment at modules/17_production/flexreg_apr16/realization.gms:13-14 ('For the time being, this approach is not applied to livestock products') is the original confabulation seed; it was not updated when M71 foragebased_jul23 landed. End-to-end chain conclusion (ruminant ↓ → feed crops ↓ → N withdrawals ↓) is still directionally correct, so demoted from Critical to Major per tie-breaker. FIXED THIS SESSION: module_17.md §1.3, §7.2, §10.5 rewritten to acknowledge M71 foragebased_jul23 behavior and flag the stale realization.gms comment. Q1-B2 (Minor, doc_error propagation, illustrative number): Sonnet's '28 kcal/day red meat in FLX-style targets' came from module_15.md L687/L980/L1460; auditor verified actual USA value in f15_targets_EATLancet_iso.cs3 is 44 kcal/day. The 28 value may originate from a paraphrase of the Willett et al. 2019 EAT-Lancet 28 g/day red meat recommendation. Presented as 'e.g.' so impact limited; deferred (auditor itself called this Minor and noted demotion uncertainty).

questions.question: lpj_runoff_minus30pct infeasibility diagnosis combining debugging_infeasibility.md + water_scarcity_scenarios.md helpers. (a) q43_water form + file:line; (b) slack/buffer term; (c) top-3 diagnostic steps; (d) EFP switch + default; (e) vm_watdem / v43_watavail / wat_src relationship.
questions.notes: Sonnet correctly identified q43_water at modules/43_water_availability/total_water_aug13/equations.gms:10-11 verbatim, presolve groundwater buffer at presolve.gms:14-16, c42_env_flow_policy default 'off', s42_env_flow_base_fraction=0.05, s42_env_flow_scenario=2 (Smakhtin), all wat_dem and wat_src set members. Strong nuance flagged: buffer activates only against exogenous (non-agricultural) demand exceedances, not against agricultural pressure — exactly the case the scenario triggers. Q2-B1 (Minor, answerer_confabulation): in (e) Sonnet's gloss said both vm_area(j,kcr,'irrigated') and vm_prod(j,kli) are 'divided by v42_irrig_eff'; per q42_water_demand (modules/42_water_demand/all_sectors_aug13/equations.gms:10-14) only the crop term is divided by v42_irrig_eff — the livestock term has v42_irrig_eff on both sides and cancels. tier_uncertainty=true (borderline Informational/Minor). Answerer issue, not doc bug.

questions.question: M44 critical-habitat extension assessment. (a) M44 risk tier per modification_safety_guide.md; (b) cropland-state interface variables; (c) circular dependencies; (d) q44_cost + vm_cost_bv_loss declaration; (e) M11 consumer + objective path.
questions.notes: Sonnet correctly identified M44 as 🟡 MEDIUM RISK (module_44.md:674), M44 absence from modification_safety_guide top-4 (10/11/17/56) and centrality top-10 (Appendix A lines 1056-1068), all three M10 interface variables (vm_land/vm_landexpansion/vm_lu_transitions) at the correct declarations.gms:14-24 lines, the C1-C4 catalog at circular_dependency_resolution.md:730-738, M44's absence from any documented cycle, q44_cost at modules/44_biodiversity/bii_target/equations.gms:28-29 with verbatim formula, vm_cost_bv_loss declared at declarations.gms:10, and the q11_cost_reg consumption path. Strong proactive flag: a new constraint tying vm_landexpansion to endogenous BII would CREATE a new simultaneous-equation cycle (M10→M30/35 BII→M44→M10), referenced safer 'lagged BII' Method 1 from circular_dependency_resolution.md:673-682. Q3-B1 (Minor, answerer_confabulation, unit-suffix fabrication): Sonnet added '/yr' to vm_cost_bv_loss units (e.g., 'mio. USD17MER/yr'); the GAMS declaration at declarations.gms:10 says just 'mio USD17MER' (no /yr), and module_44.md L94, L235 match the GAMS comment. The added suffix is semantically inferable from sibling cost variables (vm_cost_glo is 'mio. USD17MER per yr') but is not what the source actually says. tier_uncertainty=true. Answerer issue, not doc bug — no fix.

questions.question: Output interpretation pipeline. (a) report.mif vs fulldata.gdx; (b) GDX dump trigger file:line; (c) R producer function + script + IAMC naming convention; (d) why fulldata.gdx retains only final-timestep vm_* vs multi-timestep ov_*/oq_*; (e) 0.5° disaggregation script.
questions.notes: Perfect answer. All filenames correct (report.mif, fulldata.gdx), core/calculations.gms:92 verified to contain Execute_Unload, magpie4::getReport() correctly identified as producer, IAMC 'Name (unit)' naming convention correctly described, postsolve ov_*/oq_* timeseries explanation correct, scripts/output/extra/disaggregation.R correctly identified for cell.land_0.5.mz. Single Informational tier (loose range cite '46-57' instead of '53-57' for the four solution attributes — content is in the range, no point deduction). Strong proactive caveat: noted helper is silent on whether disaggregation.R also gridscales emissions (verified true — no emissions in the cell.*_0.5.mz list). LATENT DOC BUG SURFACED BY AUDIT (not in Sonnet's answer): interpreting_outputs.md:63 and :176 claim scripts/output/rds_report.R:40-45 enforce the 'Name (unit)' format; the actual rds_report.R:40 contains expectVariablesPresent() — a variable-PRESENCE check against AR6/NAVIGATE/SHAPE/AR6_MAgPIE IAMC mappings, NOT a string-format check. The format is constructed inside magpie4::getReport() itself. FIXED THIS SESSION: lines 63 + 176 rewritten to correctly attribute the format construction to magpie4::getReport and describe what rds_report.R:40-45 actually does.

questions.question: AGENT.md format compliance post-migration. (a) verbatim closing source-statement format strings; (b) verbatim 🟢 Verified line; (c) Module 17 worked examples for each; (d) why full-path form is required + which validator enforces it; (e) verbatim Step 2c 'Format' block.
questions.notes: Verbatim quote parts (a, b, e) all matched AGENT.md byte-for-byte at the cited lines (363-366, 586, 390). Worked examples in (c) used correct flexreg_apr16 realization and equations.gms:10-11 for q17_prod_reg. Q5-B1 (Major, doc_error in verifiers.md MANDATE 16): in (d) Sonnet named scripts/check_gams_citations.sh + check_gams_citations_impl.py as the validator enforcing the full-path convention. That validator only checks line-range validity against EOF — its own 'Verified by' coverage-gap line in MANDATE 16 explicitly lists 'bare-basename ambiguity silently resolved by walk-order' as a gap. The actual full-path enforcer is scripts/check_no_bare_cites.py (= Check 25 in validate_consistency.sh, added 2026-05-24 by the bare-cite migration scripts/migrate_bare_cites.py). Sonnet missed the very migration this question was probing — root cause: verifiers.md MANDATE 16 'Verified by' didn't yet reference Check 25. FIXED THIS SESSION: MANDATE 16 'Verified by' extended to list both validators with clear roles (check_gams_citations for line-range validity; check_no_bare_cites for full-path enforcement). After the fix, a future Sonnet reading the updated MANDATE 16 would name the right validator.

questions.question: What is the default realization of module 14 (yields)? List the equations defined in its equations.gms.
questions.regression_flag: stable — second consecutive 10/10. Sonnet correctly identified managementcalib_aug19 (config/default.cfg:354), exactly 2 equations q14_yield_crop (eq.gms:14-16) and q14_yield_past (eq.gms:35-39), correct dimensions and mechanism summaries. R22-corrected anchor (no q14_yieldcalib) honored. drift_observed: false.
questions.notes: Clean regression pass. Equation count is now stable; the prior spurious q14_yieldcalib reference is fully purged from agent behavior.

questions.question: Walk through how vm_carbon_stock is computed in M52 and where it enters the GHG-policy cost in M56. Cite equations and file:line.
questions.regression_flag: stable — second consecutive 10/10 (R22 G2 had 3 Minor auxiliary-list errors; R23 had zero). Sonnet led with the correct framing correction (vm_carbon_stock DECLARED in M56, not M52), correctly enumerated ALL producers (q29_carbon, q30_carbon, q31_carbon, q32_carbon, q35_carbon_primforest/secdforest/other, q59 soilc) WITH the correct populator distinction that auditors verified verbatim. Notably the R22 auxiliary-list errors (M30/M58/M59 confusion) did NOT recur — Sonnet's enumeration matched ground truth exactly. drift_observed: false.
questions.notes: Strongest G2 result to date. The framing-correction-first pattern is now reliable; producer enumeration is accurate; M56 cost chain (q56_emis_pricing_co2 → v56_emis_pricing → q56_emission_cost_oneoff → v56_emission_cost → q56_emission_costs → vm_emission_costs) walked verbatim with correct file:line cites. %c56_carbon_stock_pricing% default 'actualNoAcEst' included.

G2.trend: improving (R22: 7.0 → R23: 10.0; R22 had 3 auxiliary-list Minor bugs; R23 zero)

latent_doc_bugs_surfaced: Q4: interpreting_outputs.md:63 + :176 wrongly attributed IAMC 'Name (unit)' format enforcement to scripts/output/rds_report.R:40-45; actual lines do expectVariablesPresent variable-PRESENCE checks. Format construction is in magpie4::getReport(). FIXED.
bugs_fixed_this_session: Q1-B1 (Major, doc_error): module_17.md §1.3, §7.2, §10.5 rewritten to acknowledge that M71 foragebased_jul23 (default since 2023) populates vm_prod(j, kli_rum) and vm_prod(j, kli_mon), so vm_prod_reg(i, kli) is non-zero. Stale realization.gms:13-14 @limitations comment now explicitly flagged as out of date in the doc.

Q5-B1 (Major, doc_error in verifiers.md MANDATE 16): MANDATE 16 'Verified by' extended to list scripts/check_no_bare_cites.py (Check 25, added 2026-05-24 via bare-cite migration) alongside scripts/check_gams_citations.sh. Now identifies check_no_bare_cites.py as the actual enforcer of the full-path convention in non-module docs.

Q4 latent bug (helper-pool, doc_error): interpreting_outputs.md L63 + L176 corrected to attribute IAMC format construction to magpie4::getReport() and describe what rds_report.R:40-45 actually does (IAMC variable-presence check against AR6/NAVIGATE/SHAPE/AR6_MAgPIE mappings).
bugs_deferred: Q1-B2 (Minor): module_15.md L687/L980/L1460 illustrative '28 kcal/day red meat' number diverges from actual USA t_redmeat=44 in f15_targets_EATLancet_iso.cs3. Auditor itself flagged this as 'presented with e.g., so impact on a careful reader limited'. Deferred for a future scenario_diet_change.md refresh; not a load-bearing claim.

Q3-B1 (Minor): Sonnet's '/yr' suffix added to vm_cost_bv_loss units is an answerer-side confabulation; GAMS comment and module_44.md correctly say 'mio USD17MER' (no /yr). No fix.
highlights: Mean score 9.0/10 — new high water mark (prior best: R6 8.7, R22 8.67). First round to clear 9.0.
Zero Critical bugs (third consecutive round). Both regression anchors stable at 10/10.

G2 improvement is the headline regression signal: R22 had 3 Minor auxiliary-list errors (M30/M58/M59 confusion); R23 had zero — Sonnet's producer enumeration now matches ground truth exactly. The G2 anchor correction in R22 is propagating into agent behavior.
Q4 (interpreting_outputs.md) was a perfect 10/10. Sonnet's proactive caveat about emissions-not-being-gridscaled accurately matched the helper's silence on that topic.
Q3 (modification_safety_guide cross-module) was 9/10 with strong proactive flagging of the simultaneous-equation-cycle risk a hypothetical new constraint would create.
Q5 (AGENT.md format compliance) caught a real gap: MANDATE 16 didn't yet reference Check 25 (the validator added by THIS session's migration). The post-fix MANDATE 16 now closes that gap.

Migration coverage validated: probes targeted scenario_diet_change.md, debugging_infeasibility.md, water_scarcity_scenarios.md, interpreting_outputs.md, modification_safety_guide.md, circular_dependency_resolution.md, AGENT.md format examples, and verifiers.md MANDATE 16 — all functioned correctly except where surfaced bugs led to fixes.
